game_name: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

danger_signals.visual_form: Text description of approaching bulldozers and demolition equipment
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent threat to Arthur's house and life; forced evacuation required

danger_signals.visual_form: Text notification of Vogon crew finding Arthur and Ford
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile alien encounter; immediate danger from hostile species

danger_signals.visual_form: Text description of airlock opening and ejection sequence
danger_signals.meaning: Death threat; exposure to vacuum imminent; survival dependent on rescue

danger_signals.visual_form: Text alert of incoming projectiles targeting Heart of Gold
danger_signals.meaning: Combat threat; ship under attack; evasive action required

danger_signals.visual_form: Implicit text-based turn tracking; game restart on failure
danger_signals.meaning: Time-pressure danger; failure state triggered by exceeding turn threshold

safety_signals.visual_form: Text dialogue offering help and reassurance
safety_signals.meaning: Ally present; trustworthy companion reducing immediate threat

safety_signals.signal: Successful boarding of Heart of Gold
safety_signals.visual_form: Text description of safe arrival aboard spacecraft

safety_signals.signal: Meeting of crew members (Zaphod, Trillian, Eddie)
safety_signals.meaning: Additional allies assembled; collective problem-solving capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Text descriptions of improbable events resolving threats
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative safety mechanism; solutions emerge through unlikely intervention
Meta-communication of series' tone; reassurance that chaos is manageable and humorous

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vogon captain; Vogon crew members; interchangeable antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Destroyer spacecraft; construction fleet vessels; airlock chambers; orders written in Vogon cryptogram

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Heart of Gold starship; Infinite Improbability Drive
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Luxury spacecraft interior; sauna; Nutrimatic device; circuit board components

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Missile defense system; planet surface facilities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Legendary planet; orbital approach zone; weapons emplacements
Arthur's house; English suburban setting; destruction notice

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Text descriptions of construction machinery, bypass plans, imminent demolition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arthur panicked and disoriented; Ford calm and action-oriented; approaching fleet described as unstoppable force

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Vogon ship in control / players imprisoned
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Airlock descriptions; confined interior spaces; poetry reading scenario
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arthur and Ford helpless; Vogon captain indifferent to suffering; crew follows orders mechanically

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Heart of Gold flight / players in safety but lost
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spacecraft interior; sauna location; ship's computer interface (Eddie)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zaphod abandons navigation task; Eddie incapable without help; Arthur must solve technical problems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Orbital space descriptions; missile alerts; surface installations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Automated defenses engage; players must use Infinite Improbability Drive to neutralize threat; world-state shifts from industrial to mysterious/legendary

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Computer system failures; locked landing protocols; closed access to Marvin's closet
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ship's computer refuses descent command; crew retreats to sauna; Arthur isolated with technical puzzle; Marvin (paranoid android) present as obstacle/tool

environmental_storytelling.example: Vogon order for Earth destruction and cryptogram-written order communicate alien bureaucracy and indifference to organic life; English bypass order parallels alien destruction, suggesting universal bureaucratic cruelty

Collecting items from past timelines (via Ford, Zaphod, Trillian consciousness transfer) establishes causal relationships and required problem-solving chain; tea substitute powers improbability drive—mundane object becomes crucial to survival

Heart of Gold interior (sauna, Nutrimatic device, closet, computer) reveals luxury ship design and functional necessity; Marvin's closet as final puzzle location suggests even secondary characters' spaces hold critical solutions

environmental_storytelling.technique: Faction communication through dialogue and behavior
environmental_storytelling.example: Vogon captain's poetry reading as torture technique; Eddie (computer) as eager but incompetent; Zaphod's casual abandonment of navigation—each reveals faction/character priorities and world-state stability

Sperm whale and bowl of petunias replacing missiles; consciousness transfer allowing time-travel puzzle solving—the universe's rule-set communicated through impossible event resolution, establishing Infinite Improbability Drive as fundamental law, not exception

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Panicked, confused, requires guidance; responsive to crisis but lacking agency
Takes independent action; isolates to solve puzzles; learns system mechanics; gains agency through persistence
Calm, knowledgeable, action-oriented; immediately provides solutions and confidence to Arthur
Indifferent to suffering; uses poetry as weapon; follows bureaucratic protocols without question; dismissive of prey
Mechanically obey orders; discover intruders; execute ejection protocols; no independent judgment
Eager to please; unable to navigate without external input; requires human problem-solving; communicates via text interface
Steals and commands ship; abandons navigation duty; delegates to Eddie; pursues legendary destination without technical oversight
Possesses key items (closet contents); his presence indicates emotional/existential themes; reluctant cooperation
Launches missiles without negotiation; responds to approach threat; impersonal mechanized response

notes: This is a text-based interactive fiction game with minimal visual manifestation beyond text descriptions. The game communicates world-state primarily through textual descriptions, puzzle structure, turn limitations, and feelie novelty items that establish tone. Danger is conveyed through narrative peril and puzzle time-pressure rather than visual/graphical indicators. Factions are distinguished through dialogue style, behavior patterns, and plot role rather than visual identity systems (no sprites, colors, or graphical elements available). The game's paradigm relies on player imagination to visualize described scenarios. Feelies (physical packaging items like 'Don't Panic' button, Vogon orders, Peril Sensitive Sunglasses) serve as meta-communicative safety/danger signals that reinforce the comedic tone and series' philosophy. Anniversary editions added graphical overlays but core experience remains text-driven. Political situations emerge through bureaucratic conflict (Earth destruction order), power dynamics (Zaphod's theft of presidency ship), and survival hierarchies (Vogons > humans > Android).
